title: The tile that does not match
summary: A master tile setter in Marrakech always leaves one tile that does not match the others. He has a reason that sounds strange at first.
body:
Yusuf is a tile setter in Marrakech. He has worked with his hands for forty-one years. He makes the small coloured tiles of the old city — blue, green, yellow, white. He sets them into walls, fountains, and small floors.

A visitor came to his workshop last month. She was a young architect from Canada. She looked carefully at a small fountain Yusuf had finished. The pattern was beautiful. But she saw one tile that did not match. It was a slightly wrong green.

She said, 'Sir, there is a problem. One tile is the wrong colour.'

Yusuf smiled. He said, 'Yes. I put it there.'

She was confused. She asked, 'Why?'

Yusuf said, 'My teacher told me: Only God is perfect. The tile setter must leave one wrong tile. If he forgets, the work will think it is finished. It will stop teaching him. So I leave one wrong tile in every piece.'

The young architect did not say anything for a moment. She walked around the fountain twice. Then she said, 'Where is the wrong tile in the fountain at the city gate? I will go and find it.'

Yusuf laughed. 'You will not find it easily. I made that one when I was thirty. Back then I was proud. I put my wrong tile in a hidden place. Today I put mine where everyone can see it. The wrong tile is also part of the pattern.'

She wrote that sentence in her small notebook before she left.

annotations.meaningVi: thợ ốp gạch / thợ lát gạch
không khớp / không hợp — verb 'match' = khớp, phù hợp
Chỉ có Thượng đế là hoàn hảo
ngừng dạy anh ấy (không còn cho anh ấy học thêm điều gì)
xưởng / nơi làm việc thủ công
đi vòng quanh đài phun nước

grammarNotes.title: Reported speech with 'told' and 'said'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Tell' luôn cần đối tượng (tell + somebody + that). 'Say' không cần (say + that, hoặc say to somebody). Trong bài: 'My teacher told me, ...' (told + me). 'She said, ...' (say không có đối tượng). Quan sát: told + somebody + nội dung; said + nội dung. Đây là dạng tường thuật trực tiếp với dấu ngoặc kép. Khi chuyển sang gián tiếp, động từ trong câu thường lùi thì.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Tell' always takes an object (tell + somebody + that). 'Say' does not (say + that, or say to somebody). From the passage: 'My teacher told me, ...' (told + me). 'She said, ...' (no object). Pattern: told + somebody + content; said + content. This is direct speech (with quotation marks). In reported speech, the inner verb usually shifts back one tense.

translationVi:
Yusuf là thợ ốp gạch ở Marrakech. Anh đã làm việc bằng tay suốt bốn mươi mốt năm. Anh làm những viên gạch nhỏ nhiều màu của khu phố cổ — xanh dương, xanh lá, vàng, trắng. Anh gắn chúng vào tường, đài phun nước và sàn nhỏ.

Một khách đến xưởng của anh tháng trước. Cô là một nữ kiến trúc sư trẻ từ Canada. Cô nhìn kỹ một đài phun nước nhỏ mà Yusuf vừa làm xong. Hoa văn rất đẹp. Nhưng cô thấy một viên gạch không khớp. Nó hơi sai màu xanh lá.

Cô nói: 'Thưa ông, có một chỗ sai. Một viên gạch sai màu.'

Yusuf mỉm cười. Anh nói: 'Vâng. Tôi cố tình đặt nó ở đó.'

Cô bối rối. Cô hỏi: 'Tại sao?'

Yusuf đáp: 'Thầy tôi từng nói: Chỉ có Thượng đế là hoàn hảo. Người thợ ốp gạch phải để lại một viên sai. Nếu anh ấy quên, tác phẩm sẽ nghĩ rằng nó đã xong. Nó sẽ ngừng dạy anh ấy. Vì vậy tôi để lại một viên sai trong mỗi tác phẩm.'

Nữ kiến trúc sư trẻ im lặng một lúc. Cô đi quanh đài phun nước hai vòng. Rồi cô nói: 'Viên gạch sai trong đài phun nước ở cổng thành ở đâu? Tôi sẽ đi tìm.'

Yusuf cười. 'Cô sẽ không tìm thấy dễ đâu. Tôi làm cái đó khi tôi ba mươi. Hồi đó tôi tự kiêu. Tôi giấu viên sai của mình ở chỗ kín. Còn bây giờ tôi đặt nó ở nơi ai cũng thấy. Viên gạch sai cũng là một phần của hoa văn.'

Cô ghi câu đó vào quyển sổ nhỏ trước khi rời đi.

questions.prompt: How long has Yusuf worked as a tile setter?
questions.explanation: 'He has worked with his hands for forty-one years.'
'She was a young architect from Canada.'

questions.prompt: What did she notice on the fountain?
options: One tile in a slightly wrong green
He placed it there on purpose
'Only God is perfect. The tile setter must leave one wrong tile.'

questions.prompt: What will happen if he forgets, according to the teacher?
options: The work will think it is finished and stop teaching him

questions.prompt: What did the architect want to do after his explanation?
options: Find the wrong tile in the fountain at the city gate
questions.hint: Her response after walking around twice.

questions.prompt: Why is it hard to find the wrong tile in the city-gate fountain?
options: When younger, Yusuf was proud and hid his wrong tile in a hidden place
Because he forgot to add one
Because there is no fountain at the gate
questions.explanation: Yusuf's explanation about his younger work.

options: Now he puts the wrong tile where everyone can see it
Now he hides it more carefully
Now he leaves out more than one
Now he uses only one colour
questions.explanation: 'Today I put mine where everyone can see it.'
questions.hint: Yusuf's contrast between past and present.

questions.prompt: Which sentence does the architect write down?
options: 'The wrong tile is also part of the pattern.'
Leaving a deliberate imperfection can be an act of humility and a refusal to stop learning.
Tile setters in Morocco are not careful.
Foreign architects know more than local craftsmen.
Perfect work is the best work.
questions.explanation: The whole philosophy of the teacher.

blanks.explanation: The fountain at the city gate.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Yusuf says, 'The wrong tile is also part of the pattern.' Is there a flaw in something you have built — a project, a habit, a relationship — that you would now move from a hidden place to a visible one? What would that look like?
